{"key_event":"White Revolution and oil-financed authoritarian consolidation","reasoning":"Oil revenues, technocratic planning, SAVAK, land reform, and top-down modernization gave the monarchy stronger administrative, coercive, and rural reach than in the 1950s. Autonomous society was further constrained by repression, dissolved parliamentary checks, weakened landlords and parties, and Khomeini’s exile, though urbanization, education, bazaar-clerical networks, and underground opposition preserved some mobilizational capacity.","society_change":-0.3,"society_power":3.5,"state_change":0.8,"state_power":8.2}
